title: Bridge Essay: Bringing social interaction at the core of organizational neuroscience.

body.text: This bridge paper connects the esoteric/philosophical concept of **Social Incarnation** with mechanistic science from PubMed/MEDLINE.
**Bridge rationale:** Social Incarnation — intersubjectivity, embodied empathy, social predictive processing, second-person neuroscience.

**Bringing social interaction at the core of organizational neuroscience.** — Front Psychol. PMID: 36467198. DOI: 10.3389/fpsyg.2022.1034454. Organizations are composed of individuals working together for achieving specific goals, and interpersonal dynamics do exert astrong influence on workplace behaviour. Nevertheless, the dual and multiple perspective of interactions has been scarcely considered by Organizational Neuroscience (ON), the emerging field of study that aims at incorporating findings from cognitive and brain sciences into the investigation of organizational behaviour.
